title: Quantum Shots Parameter Not Forwarded to Backend
problem: The quantum-kc-demo workload was not accepting the shots parameter from the Quantum Control Panel, always defaulting to 50 shots instead of the user-specified value. The issue had two parts: the backend wasn't forwarding the parameter conditionally, and the results were calculating shots from count sums instead of preserving the parameter.
solution: Fix two bugs in quantum-kc-demo: (1) Remove conditional forwarding of shots parameter in web_dashboard.py line 423 to always append it, and (2) Change qapp.py result_data to use the num_shots variable instead of calculating shots from count sums.
steps: In /app/web_dashboard.py line 423, change 'if shots and shots != 10: parameters.append(f"-shots:{shots}")' to unconditionally append: parameters.append(f"-shots:{shots}")
In /app/qapp.py where result_data is built, change '"shots": sum(counts.values())' to '"shots": num_shots' to preserve the actual shots parameter used in execution
Verify the fix by querying /api/quantum/status API endpoint and confirming the shots field matches the value sent in the control panel request
Test with multiple shot values (e.g., 50, 75, 100) to ensure each returns the correct shots value in results
